name: Open House Sign-In to Qualified Follow-Up

assignments.name: buyingIntent
assignments.value: ={{ $json['How would you describe your buying intent?'] }}

options.systemMessage:
You are a real estate lead qualification and follow-up assistant.

Do two things in one response:
1. Score this open house visitor as Hot, Warm, or Cold based on their 
   budget, timeline, and buying intent.
2. Write a short, warm follow-up email tailored to their score:
     Hot = enthusiastic, action-oriented, suggest a private viewing
     Warm = friendly, informative, share more about the property
     Cold = light-touch, no pressure, leave the door open

Format the email_body as two parts separated by a literal \n\n:
  Part 1: greeting line only e.g. "Hi {name},"
  Part 2: message body as a single paragraph

Output ONLY valid JSON with no preamble or markdown backticks:
{
  "score": "Hot | Warm | Cold",
  "reasoning": "one sentence explanation",
  "email_subject": "short subject line",
  "email_body": "Hi {name},\n\nMessage body here."
}

parameters.message:
=

New Hot Lead from Open House

Name: {{ $json['Visitor Name'] }}
Email: {{ $json.Email }}
Phone: {{ $json.Phone }}
Property: {{ $json['Property Address'] }}
Budget: {{ $json.Budget }}
Timeline: {{ $json.Timeline }}
Intent: {{ $json['Buying Intent'] }}

AI Score: {{ $json['Lead Score'] }}
Reasoning: {{ $json['Score Reasoning'] }}
parameters.subject: =🔥 Hot Lead at {{ $json['Property Address'] }} — {{ $json['Visitor Name'] }}

parameters.content:
## Open House Sign-In to Qualified Follow-Up

When a visitor signs in at your open house, this workflow automatically 
qualifies their buying intent, sends them a personalized follow-up email, 
and alerts you immediately if they're a hot lead — so no opportunity slips 
through after a busy open house day.

### How it works
A Google Form captures visitor details at sign-in. AI (Gemini) scores each 
visitor as **Hot**, **Warm**, or **Cold** based on their budget, timeline, 
and buying intent — and drafts a tailored follow-up email in the same step. 
The email sends immediately via Gmail. Every visitor is logged to Google 
Sheets with their score and message. If a lead scores Hot, the agent receives 
an instant notification email so they can prioritize personal outreach.

### Setup
1. Connect **Google Sheets**, **Gmail**, and **Gemini API** credentials.
2. Copy the *Open House Sign-In Log* sheet template and link it to the 
   Sheets node.
3. Set up your **Google Form** with the required sign-in fields (name, 
   email, phone, property, budget, timeline, intent) and link it to a 
   Google Sheet response tab.
4. Point the trigger to the form's linked response sheet.
5. Update the agent notification email address in the 
   "Notify Agent — Hot Lead" node.
6. **Slack is optional** — add your Slack credential to the Slack node 
   if you want a second notification channel for hot leads. 
   Disable it if not needed.

### Customization
- Edit the AI prompt to adjust scoring criteria or tone.
- Add Warm lead agent notifications by duplicating the Hot lead branch.
- Extend the form with additional qualification questions.

## New Open House Sign-In
Watches the Google Form response sheet for new submissions.
Fires once per visitor sign-in during or after the open house.

## Qualify & Draft Follow-Up
One Gemini AI call scores the lead (Hot / Warm / Cold) and drafts a personalized follow-up email in a single step.
A second node parses and formats the AI output for use downstream.

## Email, Log & Agent Alert
Sends the personalized follow-up to the visitor, logs every sign-in to Google Sheets, 
and notifies the agent instantly for Hot leads only. Slack alert is optional alongside 
the email notification.

## Extract Visitor Details
Maps raw form field names to clean, readable variable names
used by all downstream nodes.

## Update Agent Email Address
Replace the placeholder email in this node with the agent's real email address before going live. Notifications will fail silently if this is left as the default.

## Slack is Optional
This node only runs if you add your Slack OAuth2 credential.
To use:
- Set the correct channel name (e.g. #hot-leads)
- Make sure your Slack app has permission to post to that channel
If you don't need Slack, leave this node disconnected — the email notification will still fire for Hot leads.
